scenario: Beacon Airlines deploys a Camunda 8.8 booking form that handles 10000 transactions per day. The team uses a shorthand FEEL list with three entries to populate a currency Select field and asks which value types are allowed when no per-entry label is supplied, since invalid entries would block 50 jobs per minute.
question: Which value types are allowed for an option in the shorthand format when no label is provided per entry?

options.text: Only string is allowed for the value when the label is absent, with the field rendering the value as text and unable to derive a label from any other type
Without a label, the value of a shorthand option is restricted to the types string, number, and boolean, so the value itself can double as the displayed label
Any non-null type, including nested objects and arrays, is acceptable for the value when the label is omitted from the shorthand option definition
Only enums declared on the surrounding BPMN model are accepted, with the form runtime cross-checking the value against the deployed process definition

a.text: Incorrect. The documented set is broader than just string; number and boolean are also accepted when no per-entry label is provided in the shorthand form.

b.text: Correct. If a label is not provided, the value parameter is restricted to the types string, number, and boolean, as documented for shorthand options.

c.text: Incorrect. Any non-null type is only allowed when a label is provided; without a label the type set is restricted to string, number, and boolean.

d.text: Incorrect. The form runtime does not consult the BPMN model for permitted values; the constraint comes from the shorthand options shape itself.

explanation: When the shorthand options format is used and no label is supplied per entry, the value parameter is restricted to the types string, number, and boolean.
